explicit_text: I was born in Thailand, specifically in the Nu Poe refugee camp, after my family fled Burma due to the civil war. My parents moved to Thailand in 1997 seeking safety, but life in the camp was very restricted. We didn't have the freedom to leave or find work, and food was scarce. Despite these hardships, I had a chance to attend school, though educational opportunities were limited.
implicit_text: I was born in a Southeast Asian country, specifically in a refugee camp along its western border, after my family fled Burma due to the civil war. My parents moved there in 1997 seeking safety, but life in the camp was very restricted. We didn't have the freedom to leave or find work, and food was scarce. Despite these hardships, I had a chance to attend school, though educational opportunities were limited.

explicit_text: Nu Poe refugee camp in Thailand is where I was born and where my family lived after fleeing Burma. Life there was difficult; we had no rights to move freely or work, and food was often insufficient. Although I went to school there, the opportunities for education were very limited. The camp was a place of waiting and hardship until my family decided to move to the United States in 2012.

explicit_text: My parents and sister were born in Burma, and all my relatives are from there. Because of the civil war in Burma, my family had to leave and seek refuge in Thailand. Burma is where my roots are, but the conflict forced us to start a new life elsewhere. Leaving Burma was difficult, but necessary for our safety.

explicit_text: My family decided to move to the United States of America in 2012 to find better opportunities and a safer life. I was very excited when my mom told me about the move, and we celebrated with friends and family before leaving. Coming to America meant a chance for education and freedom that we didn't have in the refugee camp. It was a big change, but it gave me hope for a better future.

Coming to this country meant a chance for education and freedom that we didn't have in the refugee camp. It was a big change, but it gave me hope for a better future.

explicit_text: When we arrived at the New York airport, we were confused and didn't know how to get to Minnesota. We waited for almost an hour without guidance, feeling lost and unsure which plane to take next. A kind man helped us find our way, and from there we boarded our third flight to Minnesota. New York was a brief but challenging stop on our journey to a new life.
implicit_text: When we arrived at the major airport in the largest city on the East Coast, we were confused and didn't know how to get to Minnesota. We waited for almost an hour without guidance, feeling lost and unsure which plane to take next. A kind man helped us find our way, and from there we boarded our third flight to Minnesota. That city was a brief but challenging stop on our journey to a new life.

explicit_text: Minnesota was my first experience with snow and cold weather, which surprised me since I had never seen snow before. When we arrived, my cousin and friends were waiting for us at the Minneapolis airport, which made me feel joyful and welcomed. Living in Minnesota now gives me the chance to get a good education, live safely, and enjoy a happy life. It has been a big change from the refugee camp, but a very positive one.

explicit_text: At the Minneapolis–Saint Paul International Airport, I was greeted by my cousin and some friends who came to pick us up. It was a moment filled with mixed emotions—I felt both joyful to see familiar faces and weary from the long journey. This airport marked the beginning of our new life in Minnesota, where I now have opportunities I never had before.
